trails.name: Puriri Track
trails.description: One of the bush walks at Mimiwhangata, the track leads you to the top of the ridge with impressive views of the peninsula and out towards the Poor Knights Islands.

trails.name: WARO TK INCL HISTORIC TRAMWAY ALIGNMENT
PUKENUI FOREST LOOP
trails.description: A family friendly walk through native forest and farmlands near Whangarei.

trails.name: PURIRI TRACK
trails.description: One of the bush walks at Mimiwhangata, the track leads you to the top of the ridge with impressive views of the peninsula and out towards the Poor Knights Islands.

trails.name: PAIHIA SCHOOL ROAD TRACK
trails.description: A pleasant walk through regenerating forest to a lookout that offers stunning views over Waitangi, the Bay of Islands and Russell.

trails.name: Te Araroa Tk - Russel Forest Section
TE HAUA URA TRACK. STREAM TRACK
CAPE BRETT TK - DEEP WATER COVE TO CAPE
PENINSULA LOOP WALK
trails.description: The Loop Walk around the headland provides a little of everything that Mimiwhangata has to offer.

trails.name: TOHUMOANA LOOKOUT TRACK
trails.description: This bush walk at Mimiwhangata offers superb views of Mimiwhangata, the coast and the Poor Knights Islands.

trails.name: OROMAHOE TRAVERSE
trails.description: This track follows a ridgeline through regenerating hardwood/podocarp forest, linking Oromahoe Road with School Road, near Paihia.

trails.name: Morepork Track
trails.description: Follow an old logging road over inland ridges through the Kaiikanui Forest.

trails.name: TE WHARA TRACK
trails.description: Follow in the footsteps of Northland's Māori ancestors. This challenging track climbs through coastal forest before revealing a spectacular 360-degree panorama. This track is part of Te Araroa Trail.

trails.name: Akeake Historic Reserve Track
trails.description: The track starts from Opito Bay and splits into a series of short, easy walks - visit a small beach in the Te Puna Inlet and a pa site on Tareha Point.

trails.name: Cape Brett Track
trails.description: The Cape Brett Track traverses rugged terrain for 16 km through native and regenerating bush. Expect dramatic coastal views; steep cliffs and drop-offs to the side of the track are a feature.

trails.name: Flagstaff Hill Track
trails.description: Enjoy 360 degree views of the Bay of Islands from this important historic site - the Union Jack was first flown here in 1840, then chopped down four times up till 1845 as a symbolic gesture against British rule.

trails.name: Horokaka Track
trails.description: Horokaka Track is a challenging track that includes strenuous climbing. The track leads up to an old logging road and then follows the ridge to the Horokaka Transmitter.

trails.name: Main Ridge Track
trails.description: Tangihua Main Ridge is a rough track, which follows the main ridge through thick vegetation. The views from the track are very rewarding on a clear day.

trails.name: Marsden Cross Track
trails.description: This walk, suitable for kids, leads across farmland to the site of the first mission settlement in New Zealand.

trails.name: Motukiore Island Walk
trails.description: This pleasant and easy walk takes you around the coastline across a causeway to Motukiore Island, and is a great summer family outing.

trails.name: Moturua Island Track
trails.description: This track goes right around Moturua Island. You can join the track at any of the main beaches and walk through open grassy areas and along cool shady stretches beneath the regenerating coastal forest trees.

trails.name: Motutara Recreation Reserve Walk
trails.description: The Motutara Recreation Reserve lies halfway between Russell and Whangārei. At the reserve you’ll find several small beaches and some good fishing and diving spots.

trails.name: Nancy Fladgate Track
trails.description: This pleasant, easy walk is a chance for children to explore the bush and the shore.

trails.name: Otaika Valley Walk
trails.description: The Otaika Valley Walk offers you a variety of landscapes including farmland and native forest.

trails.name: Peach Cove Track
trails.description: The 3-hour return walk along the Peach Cove Track requires skill and a reasonable level of fitness. The track has several steep climbs before it drops down to Peach Cove’s sandy beach.

trails.name: Ruakaka Pipeline Road Track
trails.description: Ruakaka Pipeline Road Track is considered suitable for people of most fitness levels. The 3 km track provides back dune access to Bream Bay.

trails.name: Ruapekapeka Pā Track
trails.description: Explore New Zealand's history on this short, easy walk that's suitable for buggies.

trails.name: Smugglers Bay Loop Track
trails.description: With coastal forest, spectacular views and WWII gun emplacements as well - there is plenty to keep kids interested on this short, easy walk.

trails.name: Taheke Waterfall Track
trails.description: This lovely 3-km loop track leads through a grove of regenerating kauri and offers views of the Taheke Waterfall.

trails.name: Tangihua Forest Nature Walk
trails.description: This family friendly nature walk winds through native forest and takes just over an hour one way.

trails.name: Tutukaka Head tracks
trails.description: This short, easy walk leads to the coast and a lighthouse - it's a great option for kids.

trails.name: Urupukapuka Island Walk
trails.description: This walk around Urupukupuka Island features great views - it's suitable for older children.

trails.name: Waipu Caves Track
trails.description: From the caves, a 2-km walk leads you through a picturesque karst limestone landscape across farmland and regenerating bush to a point where you can take in the magnificent views. Within the entrance to the caves themselves, you will find stalactites, sta

trails.name: Waro Limestone Scenic Reserve Walk
trails.description: Walk around spectacular limestone formations - this is a good option for kids with open grassland for exploring as well.

trails.name: Whakapono and Tumanako tracks
trails.description: The Whakapono and Tumanako tracks form a loop.  Plan for the walk to take about 2-3 hours if you wish to complete the full loop.

trails.name: Whangamumu Track
trails.description: Go for an easy forest and beach walk to Whangamumu Harbour.

trails.name: Whangaruru North Head walking tracks
trails.description: The Whangaruru North Head Walking Tracks start from the popular Puriri Bay Campsite. The tracks follow through native forest and ridgelines before dropping down into secluded bays.

trails.name: Northern Tangihua Track
trails.description: The Northern Track links up with other tracks in the Tangihua Forest.

trails.name: Te Haua Uru Track
trails.description: The Te Haua Uru Track is located in the Tangihua Forest and links with the Horokaka Track and Tangihua Main Ridge Track to form a loop that will take experienced trampers 9 hours to complete.

trails.name: Taraire Ridge Track
trails.description: A challenging walk across a ridge and through beautiful native forest.

trails.name: Mount Manaia Track
trails.description: A steep climb through regenerating native bush to the summit of Mount Manaia rewards you with breath-taking views of the dramatic entrance to Whangārei-Terenga-Paraoa (Whangārei Harbour).

trails.name: Waipu Caves Walkway
trails.description: The caves are wet and slippery inside. The inner cave area is suitable for experienced cavers only. A torch and suitable footwear are necessary.
